basic_details.short_information: The JNU Non-Teaching Staff Recruitment 2026 covers a wide range of posts from Executive Engineer to Mess Helper. Here is a at-a-glance overview of the key details candidates need before applying.

application_fee.details: The application fee for the JNU Non-Teaching Recruitment 2026 differs by post group and candidate category. Women candidates, SC, and ST applicants pay a reduced fee, while PwD candidates are fully exempted. Candidates applying for more than one post must pay the fee separately for each post, and the fee once paid is non-refundable.

age_limit.details: Candidates wondering about age relaxation for the JNU Non-Teaching Posts 2026 can note that the standard upper age limit is relaxable for reserved categories as per Government of India norms. | Category Age Relaxation SC/ST 5 Years OBC (NCL) 3 Years PwD 10 Years PwD + OBC (NCL) 13 Years PwD + SC/ST 15 Years Ex-Servicemen / ECO / SSCOs As per GoI norms Permanent Govt. / Autonomous Body employees (min. 3 years regular service) 5 Years or actual years of service (whichever is less) | There is no upper age limit for regular employees of JNU itself, provided they have completed at least three years of regular service as on the closing date of application. A relaxation of 5% in minimum qualifying marks is also available to SC, ST, OBC, and PwD candidates wherever a percentage has been prescribed.

how_to_apply: Applications for the JNU Non-Teaching Bharti 2026 are accepted only through the online portal. Candidates must complete their application before 27 July 2026 at 5:00 PM. No offline applications will be entertained under any circumstances.
Visit the official application portal at https://jnunt.samarth.edu.in
Register with a valid email ID and mobile number to create a login account.
Log in and select the post(s) you wish to apply for under Advertisement No. 01/RC(NT)/2026.
Fill in the online application form with personal, educational, and professional details accurately.
Upload scanned copies of your photograph, signature, and relevant certificates as required.
Pay the application fee online through the available payment gateway for each post applied.
Review all entries carefully and submit the application form before the deadline.
Save and print the final submitted application form for your records.

Candidates currently employed in Central/State Government, PSUs, or Autonomous Bodies must submit a No Objection Certificate (NOC) from their employer at the time of document verification or interview (wherever applicable). Failure to produce the NOC will result in cancellation of candidature.

content_sections.heading: JNU Non-Teaching Recruitment 2026 – Quick Overview
content_sections.text: The JNU Non-Teaching Staff Recruitment 2026 covers a wide range of posts from Executive Engineer to Mess Helper. Here is a at-a-glance overview of the key details candidates need before applying.

rows.information: Group A, Group B, Group C

content_sections.heading: JNU Recruitment 2026 – Important Dates
content_sections.text: Candidates planning to apply for the JNU Non-Teaching Vacancy 2026 must note that applications are accepted exclusively in online mode. The closing deadline is strict - no applications will be entertained after 27 July 2026 at 5:00 PM .

content_sections.heading: JNU Non-Teaching Vacancy 2026 – Post-Wise Breakdown
content_sections.text: The JNU Non-Teaching Staff Vacancy 2026 spans 35 different posts across Group A, B, and C categories. The total sanctioned strength is 267 posts , including backlog vacancies carried forward from previous recruitment years.

rows.post: Works Assistant (Wireman, Wireman Telephone, Carpenter, Mason, Plumber, Pump Operator, Meter Reader)

content_sections.heading: JNU Non-Teaching Recruitment 2026 – Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: Eligibility for the JNU Bharti 2026 varies significantly by post - from 10th pass for Multi Tasking Staff to postgraduate degrees with years of experience for Group A roles. Candidates must fulfil the prescribed qualification and experience as on the closing date of application. The maximum age limit for most posts is 40 years , with 45 years for Executive Engineer posts. Candidates wondering about age relaxation for the JNU Non-Teaching Posts 2026 can note that the standard upper age limit is relaxable for reserved categories as per Government of India norms. There is no upper age limit for regular employees of JNU itself, provided they have completed at least three years of regular service as on the closing date of application. A relaxation of 5% in minimum qualifying marks is also available to SC, ST, OBC, and PwD candidates wherever a percentage has been prescribed.

content_sections.heading: JNU Recruitment 2026 – Application Fee
content_sections.text: The application fee for the JNU Non-Teaching Recruitment 2026 differs by post group and candidate category. Women candidates, SC, and ST applicants pay a reduced fee, while PwD candidates are fully exempted. Candidates applying for more than one post must pay the fee separately for each post, and the fee once paid is non-refundable.

content_sections.heading: JNU Non-Teaching Recruitment 2026 Apply Online
content_sections.text: The JNU Non-Teaching Apply Online 2026 window is open and candidates must complete their submission before 27 July 2026 at 5:00 PM . Applications are accepted only at the official portal jnunt.samarth.edu.in - no offline or email submissions will be considered. For any technical queries during the application process, candidates may contact JNU at 011-26704040 or write to [email protected] . All corrigenda and updates will be published only on the official university website.

content_sections.heading: JNU Non-Teaching Recruitment 2026 – Selection Process
content_sections.text: The selection process for JNU Non-Teaching Staff Recruitment 2026 is multi-stage and varies by post group. All candidates will appear in a Computer Based Test (CBT), and shortlisted candidates proceed to subsequent stages. There is no interview for Group B and Group C posts as per MoE guidelines. Paper I is an MCQ-type written test of 2 hours carrying 300 marks across 150 questions. It covers General Awareness (30 questions, 60 marks), Reasoning Ability (35 questions, 70 marks), Mathematical Ability (35 questions, 70 marks), Test of Language - English or Hindi (30 questions, 60 marks), and Computer Awareness (20 questions, 40 marks). There is negative marking of 1/4th of the marks per wrong answer. Visually Handicapped and Cerebral Palsy candidates receive 15 minutes extra per hour. Paper II is a domain-specific MCQ test of 1.5 hours, 50 questions, and 100 marks. It applies to posts including Assistant Registrar, Section Officer, Senior Assistant, System Analyst, Assistant Librarian, Public Relation Officer, Research Officer, both Executive Engineer posts, Junior Engineer (Civil), Computer Operator, Professional Assistant, Assistant Archivist, Semi Professional Assistant, Assistant Manager (Guest House), and Statistical Assistant. A qualifying Skill/Typing Test is conducted for Private Secretary, Personal Assistant, Stenographer, Assistant, and Junior Assistant. This test is qualifying in nature and does not add to the merit score. For all Group A posts, an Interview/Personality Test worth 100 marks is conducted. The final merit is calculated with 70% weightage for the written examination (Paper I + Paper II) and 30% for the Interview. Candidates are called for interview at a ratio not exceeding 1:15 (vacancies to candidates).

content_sections.heading: JNU Non-Teaching Recruitment 2026 – Salary and Pay Scale
content_sections.text: All selected candidates in the JNU Non-Teaching Staff Vacancy 2026 will receive pay as per the 7th Pay Commission Pay Matrix, plus allowances as admissible under University rules. The pay level for each post is clearly defined in the official notification.

Semi Professional Assistant, Assistant Manager (Guest House), Statistical Assistant
Multi Tasking Staff, Mess Helper, Engineering Attendant, Laboratory Attendant, Library Attendant, Animal Attendant, Groundsman, Anti-Malaria Worker

content_sections.heading: JNU Non-Teaching Recruitment 2026 – How to Apply Online
content_sections.text: Applications for the JNU Non-Teaching Bharti 2026 are accepted only through the online portal. Candidates must complete their application before 27 July 2026 at 5:00 PM . No offline applications will be entertained under any circumstances. Candidates currently employed in Central/State Government, PSUs, or Autonomous Bodies must submit a No Objection Certificate (NOC) from their employer at the time of document verification or interview (wherever applicable). Failure to produce the NOC will result in cancellation of candidature.

faq.question: Q1. Has JNU officially released the Non-Teaching Recruitment 2026 notification?
faq.answer: Yes, Jawaharlal Nehru University has officially released the Non-Teaching Recruitment 2026 notification under Advertisement No. 01/RC(NT)/2026. The notification is available at www.jnu.ac.in/career.

faq.question: Q2. What is the total number of vacancies in JNU Non-Teaching Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The total number of vacancies is 267 across 35 different posts in Group A, Group B, and Group C categories, including backlog vacancies from previous recruitment years.

faq.question: Q3. What is the last date to apply for JNU Non-Teaching Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application is 27 July 2026 at 5:00 PM. No applications will be accepted after this deadline.

faq.question: Q4. What is the application fee for JNU Non-Teaching posts 2026?
faq.answer: For Group A posts, the fee is ₹1,500 for UR/EWS/OBC and ₹1,000 for SC/ST/Women candidates. For Group B and C posts, the fee is ₹1,000 for UR/EWS/OBC and ₹600 for SC/ST/Women. PwD candidates are fully exempted across all groups.

faq.question: Q5. What is the age limit for JNU Non-Teaching Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The maximum age limit is 40 years for most posts and 45 years for Executive Engineer posts. Age relaxation is applicable as per Government of India norms for SC/ST (5 years), OBC-NCL (3 years), and PwD (10 years) categories.

faq.question: Q6. Is there an interview for Group B and Group C posts in JNU Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: No. As per the Ministry of Education guidelines, there is no interview for Group B and Group C posts. Selection is based on the written test and qualifying skill test (wherever applicable).

faq.question: Q7. Where can I apply for JNU Non-Teaching Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Applications must be submitted online only at the official portal jnunt.samarth.edu.in. No offline applications are accepted.

faq.question: Q8. What is the selection process for Group A posts in JNU Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: For Group A posts, the selection involves Paper I (General Test), Paper II (Domain-specific), and an Interview/Personality Test. The final merit is drawn with 70% weightage for the written exam and 30% for the interview.

faq.question: Q9. What is the minimum qualification for Junior Assistant in JNU 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must hold a Bachelor's Degree from any recognized university or institute, along with proficiency in typing at 35 wpm in English or 30 wpm in Hindi, and basic computer operation skills.

faq.question: Q10. Is negative marking applicable in the JNU Non-Teaching Exam 2026?
faq.answer: Yes. There is negative marking of 1/4th of the marks allocated per question for wrong answers in both Paper I and Paper II.

faq.question: Has JNU officially released the Non-Teaching Recruitment 2026 notification?
faq.answer: Yes, Jawaharlal Nehru University has officially released the Non-Teaching Recruitment 2026 notification under Advertisement No. 01/RC(NT)/2026. The notification is available at www.jnu.ac.in/career.
